dependencies: Raw materials essential to business include proppants (primarily sand), chemicals, metals, gels, and electronic components (circuit boards)
Market conditions can trigger constraints in supply of certain raw materials
Extended supply chain lead times for select raw materials
Reliance on rail, storage, and trucking services to transport raw materials to job sites
risks: Shortage of raw materials due to high levels of demand or loss of suppliers during market challenges
Single supplier relationships for particular resources
Transportation service delays during times of high demand may constrain supply of raw materials
Inflationary cost increases primarily related to chemicals, cement, and logistics costs
Challenges to source sufficient electric power for Zeus electric fracturing systems
Inadequate infrastructure to support operation of electric fracturing systems

regulatory_risks: Compliance with United States Foreign Corrupt Practices Act (FCPA)
Anti-corruption laws in multiple jurisdictions
Import and export control laws and regulations
Economic sanctions against certain countries, persons, and entities
Trade barriers and tariffs
Changes in U.S. foreign trade policies and potential new tariffs
Currency controls and repatriation restrictions in certain countries
Environmental laws and regulations imposing strict liability
Cleanup costs and natural resource damages under environmental laws
Superfund site liabilities
Hazardous materials and waste disposal regulations
Radioactive and explosive materials regulations
Underground storage tanks and injection wells regulations
Worker safety regulations (onshore and offshore)
Greenhouse gas and climate change regulations
Federal, state, and local laws on hydraulic fracturing
Chemical disclosure requirements for hydraulic fracturing
Restrictions or bans on hydraulic fracturing in certain jurisdictions
Seismic activity-related restrictions on operations
Mine Safety and Health Administration regulations
Pillar Two global minimum tax framework
IRS Notice of Proposed Adjustment (NOPA) on $3.5 billion termination fee classification

sanction_risks: Russia sanctions led to disposal of Russian operations in 2022
Sanctions on Russia in connection with Ukraine invasion
Economic sanctions may limit or prevent business in certain jurisdictions
Potential sanctions on countries, persons, and entities restricting transactions
Risk of government actions resulting in expropriation and nationalization of assets
Confiscatory taxation or adverse tax policies in foreign jurisdictions
Currency controls and restrictions on cash repatriation (e.g., Argentina, Egypt)
Political and economic instability including civil unrest, terrorism, war, and armed conflict
Ongoing actions in Ukraine, Israel, and broader Middle East
Trade barriers, tariffs, and restrictions on transport of goods and equipment
